title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple asserts that a murder did not occur at Gossington Hall, but that the body was moved there. During a conversation with Heather and Arthur Badcock, Heather discusses her admiration for actress Marina Greg, recounting a time she met the star despite being ill. Miss Marple notes that Heather reminds her of a woman named Eleanor Wilder, later revealing to Heather that her friend Alison Wilder is dead. The conversation shifts to a memory of Arthur Badcock lamenting the loss of antique Georgian teaspoons that Heather once gave to a family from a farmhouse.

In a separate scene, Mrs. Bantry hosts Miss Marple at her small house near the east gate of Gossington Hall. Mrs. Bantry explains that she sold the main Hall and much of its land following the death of her husband, Colonel Bantry. She discusses the Hall's history of failed ownership, including its time as a hotel and its division into apartments. The two women discuss Marina Greg, who has purchased Gossington Hall with her husband, a film producer or director. Mrs. Bantry notes that Marina's career declined following a breakdown after her divorce. Miss Marple shares her observations on the pressures of being in the public eye and recalls meeting Marina in California. They discuss Marina's history of multiple marriages, including a romantic union with actor Robert Trask and a marriage to playwright Isidore Wright, which resulted in a child. Miss Bettoncourt suggests that Marina and her husband bought the Hall to be closer to Elstree.
